title: USDA says prepared, data says burned — the wildfire readiness gap nobody is measuring
narrative: Agriculture Secretary issued a May 2026 Wildfire Readiness Memorandum claiming to build on "progress" made in 2025. The USDA's 2026 Fire Letter of Intent claims to "strengthen wildfire response and reduce risk across landscapes." The Forest Service dashboard shows 1.5M–2M acres treated for hazardous fuels. Officials point to the memo as evidence of preparedness.
telemetry: NPR (May 4, 2026): prescribed burning FELL from 1.6M acres (2023, 2024) to ~900,000 acres in 2025 — a 44% reduction
Forest Service's own fiscal year data: 1 million fewer acres burned in prescribed fires vs. prior years
NIFC (National Interagency Coordination Center) May 9, 2026 sitrep: nearly 23,000 fires already burned >1.8 million acres in 2026
2026 acreage burned in 5 months exceeds the 10-year average for the same period
2025 peer-reviewed study: prescribed fire reduces wildfire burn severity 16% on average, lowers net smoke emissions 14%
analysis: The administration runs a communication gap between 'we're prepared' messaging and hard data showing a dramatic reduction in the one tool (prescribed burns) that reduces wildfire severity. The 1.8M acres already burned is the physical evidence. You cannot simultaneously claim full preparedness and admit prescribed burning fell by 44%. Both cannot be true at once. The acreage data is public — why isn't anyone cross-checking?
verdict: [SIPHONED VERDICT]: Prescribed burns down 44%. Acres burned up dramatically. The dashboard says 'progress.' The land says otherwise.
